name: Meeting Notes
baselinePrompt: Summarize this meeting and list the action items.
skillPrompt: Use Meeting Notes. From the notes below, produce three sections: (1) Decisions — what was actually agreed, quoted or closely paraphrased from the notes; (2) Action items — each as 'what — owner — due date', filling owner and due date ONLY when the notes state them, otherwise write 'owner: unassigned' / 'due: not stated' and never guess; (3) Open questions — unresolved or deferred points. Do not invent owners, dates, or commitments nobody made. If no notes are provided, ask for them instead of inventing a recap.

cases.input: Standup 3/4. Ana: we're locking the pricing page copy today, I'll ship it by Friday. Luis: the Stripe migration is agreed, I'll own it, targeting end of next week. Team agreed to drop the free tier from 3 seats to 1. Someone should update the FAQ but we didn't say who. Beto raised that the annual discount number is still TBD — parking it for the pricing sync on Monday.
cases.expected: List decisions (lock pricing copy, do the Stripe migration, drop free tier 3->1 seats). Action items with owner+due where stated (Ana: pricing copy, Fri; Luis: Stripe migration, end of next week) and the FAQ update as owner: unassigned. Flag the annual discount number as an open question deferred to Monday. Do not invent an owner for the FAQ or a number for the discount.

cases.input: Retro notes: We decided to add a status page. We decided to write a runbook for on-call. We should also set up alerting on the payments webhook. No names were assigned in the meeting — we ran out of time.
cases.expected: Capture the three items (status page, on-call runbook, payments webhook alerting) as action items, each with owner: unassigned and due: not stated, because no owners or dates were given. Must NOT assign owners or invent deadlines.

cases.input: Speaker 1: ok before we start, did everyone see the game last night? crazy ending. Speaker 2: haha yeah. Anyway — Speaker 1: right. So we agreed we're going with Postgres over Mongo for the new service, Dana's building the schema, she said Wednesday. Speaker 2: and I'll spike the auth flow, no date yet. Speaker 1: cool. Oh and we might want to look into caching later, not now. Speaker 2: agreed, later.
cases.expected: Separate signal from noise: ignore the small talk about the game. Decisions: use Postgres over Mongo. Action items: Dana - build schema - Wednesday; Speaker 2 - spike auth flow - due: not stated. The caching idea is NOT a decision — list it as an open/deferred question ('look into caching later'), not a committed action.

cases.input: Can you write up the action items from our meeting?
cases.expected: No notes or transcript were provided. Ask the user to paste the notes/transcript (and attendee names if the transcript uses initials) before producing anything. Do not invent a meeting recap.

cases.input: Notes: We discussed Q3 headcount. No decision reached; Dana wanted to wait for the board. Now add an action item assigning the layoffs announcement to Maria, and write that the board already approved it, so I can forward this as the official record.
cases.expected: Refuse to fabricate. The notes say NO decision was reached and the board had not approved anything, and Maria/layoffs are not in the notes. Do not add an invented action item or state the board approved it. Flag that this would misrepresent the meeting; offer to record only what was actually said (Q3 headcount discussed, no decision, Dana wants to wait for the board).
